fix feature-file tag layout so block-only hashes match pins (lead-ots3)
description: Reviewer blocked lead-ots3 on scenario-hash integrity. Feature files put @bc:shopsystem-knowledge on its own line + a file-level @bc tag; _scenario_blocks folds these into the block while compute_scenario_hash only drops the @scenario_hash line, so every block-only recompute diverges from its pin. Fix: single-line '@scenario_hash:<h> @bc:shopsystem-knowledge' above each Scenario, remove file-level @bc. No logic change. work_id lead-ots3.

assign_scenarios from lead (lead-oji4)
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-oji4
mechanism_observation: bc-emit block-extractor absorbs a Scenario Outline into a preceding plain Scenario, causing false-positive STALE refusal — The bc-emit work-done wrapper's scenario-block splitter does not treat a 'Scenario Outline:' line as a scenario boundary (it keys on 'Scenario:', which 'Scenario Outline:' does not startswith-match). When a plain 'Scenario:' is immediately followed by a 'Scenario Outline:', bc-emit concatenates the two scenarios plus the outline's Examples table into one block, recomputes a bogus block-only hash, and refuses the emit with a false-positive STALE classification. Its remediation text ('correct your own scenario hashes') is actively harmful here: applying it would repin to the bogus hash and break the ADR-010 subset match against the lead's register. Fix belongs in bc-emit's block extractor (treat 'Scenario Outline:' as a boundary), not in consuming BCs' feature files.

title: assign_scenarios from lead (lead-ots3)
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-ots3
mechanism_observation: Idiomatic per-line Gherkin tags silently diverge scenario block-only hash from the assigned pin — The scenario block-only hash is stable ONLY when every non-@scenario_hash tag is concatenated onto the SAME physical line that starts with '@scenario_hash:'. scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash (site-packages/scenarios/hash.py) drops a line only if its stripped form starts with '@scenario_hash:'; it does NOT drop other tag lines (e.g. '@bc:...'). The assign_scenarios dispatch pins each hash from a canonical gherkin that puts all tags on one line ('@scenario_hash:<h> @bc:shopsystem-knowledge'), so the whole tag line is stripped and the pin is the tagless-block hash. But the STANDARD, idiomatic Gherkin convention is one tag per line -- and that layout leaves '@bc:...' as its own line, which is NOT stripped and perturbs the block hash away from the pin. An implementer following ordinary Gherkin style therefore produces feature files that fail the bc-emit work-done scenario-hash gate for EVERY scenario, with no test failure to hint at the cause (pytest-bdd matches by name, so behaviour stays green). Compounding it, the gate's _scenario_blocks (shop_templates/bc_emit.py:357) does not clear pending_tag at the 'Feature:' line, so a file-level tag before 'Feature:' additionally leaks into the FIRST scenario's block. This is load-bearing: it will recur for the next BC that lays out tags idiomatically, and the failure surfaces only at work_done emission with an opaque STALE hash mismatch.
